short_reason: Strong hook, real breach examples, and engaging tone. However, the article uses styled HTML divs as section headers instead of proper markdown H2/H3 headings, which breaks logical heading hierarchy and is a significant structural/SEO issue. The OpSec Vault CTA is also overtly promotional, reducing editorial credibility.
red_flags: Section headers are styled HTML divs rather than proper markdown H2/H3, breaking logical heading hierarchy and SEO structure
No meta description present in YAML frontmatter, limiting SERP click-through appeal
OpSec Vault promotional CTA embedded mid-article is overtly commercial and undermines editorial credibility
The 2022 payment processor breach and 2023 AWS key incident could benefit from verified sources/attribution
No conclusion or closing section — the article ends abruptly after the secure alternative paragraph
